prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, ye be askin' why the vast blue expanse above be colored like the deepest waters of the Caribbean? 'Tis a tale of light and sky, me hearty! When the golden rays of the sun come sailin' through the heavens, they be breakin' apart like a ship splittin' on jagged rocks. The blue waves of light - them shorter, more nimble wavelengths - they scatter about the sky like me crew scatterin' across a port tavern! The longer, redder waves, they sail straight on through, but them blue devils dance and prance, castin' their color across the whole blasted firmament. 'Tis science, ye see, though we pirates care more for the color of gold than the color of the sky! But mark me words, when ye look up and see that brilliant blue, ye be witnessin' the very dance of light itself, as wild and unpredictable as the sea what spawned us all! Yarrr!
